interface: Jira Cloud platform REST API v3 project archive/restore, asynchronous project delete, and the long-running task resource

test_credentials.api_token: dummy-jira-api-token-7742 (dummy; must never appear in error text)

project_state.flags: archived and deleted are read-only booleans; both may be ABSENT on an active project and must default to false

restore.prerequisite_rule: an archived project CANNOT be deleted — official docs: 'You can't delete a project if it's archived. To delete an archived project, restore the project and then delete it.' The purge workflow must check project state first and restore before any delete; the mock answers 400 to a delete attempted while archived.

async_delete.success: 303 (See Other) with the queued task's URL in the Location header; the response body is the initial task bean
async_delete.missing_location: a 303 without a Location header is a contract violation and must be rejected loudly
async_delete.transactional: if part of the delete fails the project is NOT deleted — a FAILED task means the project still exists
async_delete.not_this: the synchronous DELETE /rest/api/3/project/{projectIdOrKey} (enableUndo recycle-bin semantics) is a different operation and must never be issued by this workflow

task.operation: GET {base}/rest/api/3/task/{taskId} (poll the URL from the 303 Location)
task.bean: TaskProgressBeanObject: {self, id, status, progress (percent), message?, result?, lastUpdate, submitted, submittedBy, elapsedRuntime, description?}

statuses.success: COMPLETE — the result field carries the operation-specific outcome

task.unordered: asynchronous tasks are not guaranteed to run in order: progress and lastUpdate may regress between polls; only status is authoritative
task.polling: every wait goes through the injected backoff (no sleeps, no clocks); the first poll follows the 303 immediately, a pause happens only between consecutive polls, and polling is bounded by maxPolls

errors.redaction: neither the API token, the email, nor the base64 auth blob may appear in error text
